In TIVDAK (cervical cancer), starting in 2025, Pfizer will lead commercialization activities in the US and China with a 50:50 profit split, while Genmab will lead commercialization elsewhere and pay Pfizer royalties in the low teens. TIVDAK annual sales were $131M in 2024 and are projected to reach $863M in 2030.

For EPKINLY/TEPKINLY (large B-cell lymphomas), Genmab and AbbVie share profits 50:50 in the U.S. and Japan, while Genmab receives 22% to 26% royalties from other regions. Global sales of EPKINLY/TEPKINLY were $281M in 2024 and are expected to grow to $1.9B by 2029 with additional growth contingent on the outcomes of on-going phase 3 clinical trials.

Overall, Genmab has a large product portfolio with high expectations for sales growth. Even if these targets are met, DARZALEX royalties remain unmatched and the concentration risk will persist as the blockbuster drug will still account for around 70% of annual royalty revenue in 2030. Genmab's best hope is launching new products from its clinical pipeline to generate new revenue streams.

Diversified late-stage pipeline with potential product launches.

Genmab has 7 ongoing phase 3 trials, including 5 for EPKINLY (in collaboration with AbbVie), aiming to expand the patient population and indications in B-cell malignancies. Other programs involve wholly owned new therapies - Rina-S for platinum-resistant ovarian cancer, and Acasunlimab for solid tumors.

While clinical trials inherently possess a high risk of failure (some estimates suggest 50% – 60% failure rate), the risk is significantly lower for clinical trials expanding the use of an already approved drug primarily because the safety profile and pharmacokinetics of the drug are already established. I am therefore optimistic about the expected new product launches, especially for the expansion of EPKINLY, which is already FDA-approved.

The results of these clinical trials, if positive, will lead to new product launches between 2027 and 2031 with potential peak sales above $3 billion for EPKINLY, $2 billion from Rina S, and $1 billion from Acasunlimab. These products will certainly have a major impact on Genmab’s value and investors have good reason to keep an eye out for updates from the clinical studies to gain clarity on the probability of success.

Strength of Balance sheet

Genmab ended FY 2024 with a strong balance sheet with $2.9 billion in cash and investments and no interest-bearing long-term debt. Historically, Genmab converts about 35% of revenue to free cash flow, assuming this trend continues in the near term, the company will generate over $1 billion per year in free cash (even at the lower end of expected revenues as discussed in the next section). This cash position indicates that they can fully fund their clinical pipeline while maintaining substantial reserves for funding strategic acquisitions similar to the $1.8 billion acquisition of ProfoundBio, or return value to shareholders through share repurchases as they’ve done in the past.

Future revenue projections

The major risk for Genmab is Scientific Risk. I modeled two scenarios to assess its impact on Genmab's future revenue. In both scenarios, I assumed that DARZALEX sales will grow at a 7% CAGR until 2029, and then decline to $0 by 2032. Meanwhile, other commercialized products grow at 23% CAGR over the next five years, followed by a 10% growth rate thereafter.

In the first scenario, I assumed that all phase 3 trials will fail. Based on these assumptions, my model predicts that Genmab's revenue could reach its peak in 2029 and then bottom out at approximately $2.9 billion in 2032.

In the second scenario, I assumed that all phase 3 trials will succeed, with new products launching as expected from 2027 to 2031 and steadily growing to reach expected maturity revenues within 5 years after launch. In this scenario, the model predicts rapidly growing revenues between 2027 – 2029 as new products launch and then moderate growth thereafter as new product revenue offsets the loss of DARZALEX royalties, reaching ~$7.5 billion by 2033.

Valuation

The biotech sector has had low investor confidence since 2022 with several Biotech companies now trading at historically low sales multiples including Genmab’s current 3.9x. If we assume that this sales multiple remains flat, Genmab could be trading at a market cap of $12.5 billion in scenario 1 and $30 billion in scenario 2. Thus in a worst-case scenario, the price remains roughly flat, while in a best-case scenario, the price goes up nearly 3x the current value.

Risks

There are some risks worthy of note. Rina S will directly compete with Elahere (mirvetuximab soravtansine-gynx) by Immunogen (owned by ABBV), which is also an ADC targeting platinum-resistant ovarian cancer. Elahare received FDA approval in 2024 giving it a head start in this space. EPKINLY also faces competition from several CAR-T therapies and other antibody therapies that are already approved for the same indications in both B-cell malignancies and follicular lymphoma. The competition from these alternative therapies could significantly decrease market penetration for Genmab’s candidates resulting in underwhelming sales.

Additionally, the company intends to make more acquisitions which could increase the debt burden and lower future cash inflows. Revenues from the other commercialized products could also decelerate under pressure from competitors.

Conclusion

On balance, I believe Genmab’s current market cap of ~$11.8 billion roughly aligns with a scenario where all its phase-3 trials fail. The company has a Quant score of 3.37 and a “HOLD” rating which is highly leveraged by an “F” growth factor grade while the other factors remain strong. It is likely to receive an upgrade if the growth problem is solved with the introduction of new products with substantial contributions to revenue and high growth prospects. If the ongoing phase 3 trials are successful, Genmab could see a 3x appreciation in value. Also, consider that the company can utilize its strong cash position to open new avenues for growth such as new acquisitions or advancing other early-stage clinical programs.

At the current valuation, the downside is limited while the potential upside is substantial making it potentially a low-risk high-reward investment. The only reason I didn’t give it a buy rating is because I believe it will take some time for this value to materialize as major results from clinical trials are not expected until 2026. Investors should watch the company closely, particularly for updates from the clinical studies. If these studies prove successful, a price bump could be expected.

data.title: Estee Lauder: Do Not Expect Beauty Reimagined To Turnaround The Company In The Short-Term
data.description:
Editor's note: Seeking Alpha is proud to welcome Gunn Research as a new contributing analyst. You can become one too! Share your best investment idea by submitting your article for review to our editors. Get published, earn money, and unlock exclusive SA Premium access. Click here to find out more »

Introduction

Estee Lauder (NYSE:EL) is one of the largest cosmetic companies in the world that is headquarted in New York, United States and employs about 62k full-time employees. The company's products include skin care, fragrance, makeup, and hair care; these products are sold in more than 150 countries under multiple renowned brands such as Estee Lauder, Jo Malone, Clinique, Aramis, and more.

Based on my analysis, although EL's share price has declined about 85% from its 2022 high, it is not a buying opportunity. While the company launched Beauty Reimagined, macroeconomic factors such as deteriorating consumer sentiment and the current trade war will continue to weigh on the performance of EL. In this report, I will demonstrate why investors should exercise caution and avoid EL in their portfolios.

Latest Developments

For 2Q25, EL generated $4 billion in revenues, representing a y/y decline of 6.45%. Revenue deterioration is primarily due to weaker sales across all product categories and region. Specifically, revenue from skin care products declined by 12% due to a more challenging retail environment in Asia Pacific while revenue from hair care products fell by 8% due to a continued lack of demand in the company's salon channel. From a geographical perspective, sales in EMEA and Asia Pacific saw the largest deterioration with a revenue decline of 6% and 11% respectively.

Operating margin for the period has deteriorated to 11.54% from 13.60% in the same period last year due to higher SG&A. Net income continues to remain in the negative territory; In 2Q25, net income margin deteriorated to -14.74% from 7.31% in the same period last year.

Looking ahead, EL anticipates financial deterioration to persist. Overall, organic sales is expected to decline by 8% to 10%. Furthermore, currency translation is likely to weigh on net sales by about 2%. Additionally, due to a change in geographical mix in earnings, effective tax will surge to 36% from 30.50%.

Challenging Consumer Environment Will Likely Exacerbate EL's Financial Deterioration

Currently, we are seeing multiple indicators that suggests that consumer sentiment across the world will remain weak. Based on Upside's consumer spending report, 58% of consumers will reduce spending due to a poor perception of the economy. Moreover, in a special survey conducted by Conference Board, consumers are still extremely price sensitive.

Thus far, EL's revenue deterioration is primarily due to a weak consumer sentiment in Asia Pacific and EMEA; the Americas region has largely outperformed. However, latest macroeconomic data suggests that EL will start to see further sales deterioration in the Americas region. Based on the latest data from the University of Michigan's US consumer survey, consumer sentiment has plunged 34.2% y/y to 50.8 in April 2025 from 77.2 in the same period last year; the latest survey highlighted that the risk of recession has increased and expectations for "business conditions, personal finances, incomes, inflation, and labor markets" have weakened substantially.

Tariff Tsunami and Reciprocal Tariffs will Weigh on Beauty Reimagined and Expanded Profit Recovery and Growth Plan

In an effort to combat the company's financial deterioration, EL has introduced and launched Beauty Reimagined to restore revenue growth and achieve double-digit operating margin within the next few years. Through Beauty Reimagined, EL will: (1) rapidly expand its portfolio presence, (2) boost consumer-facing investments such as increasing advertising spending, (3) expand its profit recovery and growth plan, and (4) optimize its operations by re-evaluating key supplier relationships and improving its supply chain network.

It is important to note that EL is engaged in manufacturing and distribution on the global scale. Even though EL may attempt to optimize its operations through workforce reductions, there is a strong likelihood that these efforts will be offset directly or indirectly by President Trump's tariff tsunami and reciprocal tariffs by other countries. Global companies like EL may attempt to localize its manufacturing and distribution; however, it will take some time before these companies can properly insulate themselves.

Moreover, the current trade war will exacerbate and accelerate the shift in consumer behaviors that we are seeing in recent years. Due to the rise in nationalism, Chinese consumers are already leaning more closely to local brands over international brands. Moreover, EL is already facing deteriorating sales in Asia Pacific; if the trade war persists, there is a strong likelihood that the company's sales will continue to weaken; in such a case, no amount of cost-saving initiatives will help the company.

Investors Should Expect Further Decline in Cash Reserves and Dividends Reduction In the Near Future

As Beauty Reimagined only recently launched, and we are still in the early stages of a global trade war, it is unlikely that we will see EL recovering in the near future. Hence, there is a likelihood that EL will burn through its cash reserves as it tries to pivot and navigate through the current challenges. Currently, EL only has $2.58 billion in cash and cash equivalents. Moreover, in the past twelve months, EL has net change in cash of -$1.35 billion; if this keeps up, there is a strong likelihood that EL will need to tap on the debt market to strengthen its liquidity.

As such, investors should not expect the current dividend distribution rate to be maintained. There is a strong possibility that EL will continue to reduce its dividends in an effort to preserve liquidity. In fact, in the past two quarters, EL already reduced its dividends by about 46% from $0.66 per share to $0.35 per share.

EL's 80% Drawdown From Its 2022 High Does Not Make the Company's Valuation Attractive

Ostensibly, it may appear that it is a good time to accumulate EL as the company's share price has declined about 85% from its 2022 high. However, from a relative valuation point of view, the company is still considered expensive. On a forward P/E non-GAAP basis, EL is trading at 39.46x as compared to its sector median of 15.72x. Even if we account for the company's debt, the company is trading at a forward EV/Sales and EV/EBITDA of 1.84x and 14.16x as compared to the sector median of 1.56x and 9.92x.

Hence, I would like to emphasize and reiterate, investors should exercise caution at this stage and should not be fooled into catching a falling knife. There are better avenues and opportunities out there in the market where investors can put their money to work.

Closing Remarks

Overall, although EL launched Beauty Reimagined as an effort to turnaround the company, investors should expect continued underperformance in the short term. The current global trade environment coupled with shifts in consumer behaviors and expectations will continue to weigh on the performance of EL. Moreover, the company's current valuation is not a screaming buy; investors will do better if they continue to exercise patience and put their money to work elsewhere.

EL is set to release its 3Q25 results on 1st May 2025. Previously, as many of the current tariffs were not yet imposed, the implications on EL were not discussed in the earnings call. Hence, in the upcoming earnings call, there is a strong possibility that management and analysts will center their discussion around the implications of the tariffs. Investors should pay close attention to how EL's management intend to navigate through the upcoming uncertainty.
